title: Meeting Output
description: Open, portable schema for meeting recordings processed into structured outputs (transcript, summary, action items, decisions). Designed to be tool-agnostic so any local-first transcription pipeline can produce or consume it.

schema_version.description: Version of this schema the document conforms to. Follows semver. Consumers MUST check this field before parsing.

meeting_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this meeting output. ULID is RECOMMENDED (sortable by creation time, 26 chars, Crockford base32). UUIDs are also accepted.

generated_at.description: When this document was produced, as RFC 3339 timestamp with timezone. Distinct from source.recorded_at.

source.description: Information about the original recording. Treat as the input side of the pipeline.

file_name.description: Basename of the source file as seen by the user. Path-stripped to avoid leaking filesystem layout.

sha256.description: SHA-256 hash of the source audio file content (lowercase hex). Anchors chain of custody: any downstream artifact can be tied back to the exact bytes that produced it.

format.description: Container/codec name as reported by ffprobe (e.g. 'mp4,m4a', 'wav', 'mp3').

duration_sec.description: Duration of the source audio in seconds.

size_bytes.description: Size of the source file in bytes.

sample_rate_hz.description: Audio sample rate in Hz, e.g. 44100, 48000.

codec.description: Audio codec name as reported by ffprobe (e.g. 'aac', 'pcm_s16le').

recorded_at.description: When the recording was captured, if known (RFC 3339). May come from file mtime, container metadata, or user input. Distinct from generated_at.

language.description: BCP 47 language tag detected or specified for the recording (e.g. 'en', 'ru', 'en-US'). Use 'und' if undetermined.

text.description: Full transcript as plain text. Convenience field; canonical content lives in segments.

segments.description: Ordered list of transcript segments. The unit of granularity for source_segment_ref pointers from action_items and decisions.

summary.description: Free-form meeting summary produced by an LLM.

text.description: Summary as plain text or lightweight Markdown.

action_items.description: Concrete tasks identified in the meeting that require follow-up.

decisions.description: Final decisions agreed upon during the meeting.

participants.description: Known meeting participants. May be empty if speaker identification was not performed.

provenance.description: Chain of custody: which tools, models, and configuration produced this document. Critical for trust and reproducibility. Consumers can use this to verify or reproduce results.

producer.description: The software that generated this document.

pipeline.description: Per-stage tool and model identification. Each stage is optional so that partial pipelines (e.g. transcript-only) are valid.

transcriptSegment.description: A single time-aligned chunk of transcript.

id.description: Stable identifier for this segment within the document. Recommended format: 'seg_NNNN' zero-padded. Used as target for source_segment_ref.

start_sec.description: Segment start time in seconds from the beginning of the recording.

end_sec.description: Segment end time in seconds. Must be greater than or equal to start_sec.

speaker_id.description: Reference to participants[].id if diarization was performed. Null if speaker is unknown.

confidence.description: Optional ASR confidence score in [0, 1]. Null if the engine does not expose it.

is_approximate_timing.description: True if start_sec/end_sec are not real timecodes from the ASR engine but were synthesized (e.g. line-based fallback). Default false. Allows consumers to know when timing is reliable.

actionItem.description: A task to be done after the meeting.

id.description: Stable identifier within the document. Recommended format: 'act_NNNN'.

assignee.description: Free-form name or handle. May reference participants[].name when speaker resolution exists, but is not constrained to it (LLMs may name people not present).

due_date.description: Original phrasing of the deadline as it appeared in the meeting (e.g. 'by end of week', 'next Friday'). Preserves the user's intent even when not parseable to a calendar date.

due_date_iso.description: Normalized deadline as ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD), if it could be resolved. Null when due_date is fuzzy or absent.

status.description: Lifecycle status. Defaults to 'open' for newly extracted items. Forward-compatible field; consumers may extend.

source_segment_ref.description: Transcript segment IDs that support this action item. Provides chain of evidence: each item can be traced back to the audio segments that produced it. May be empty if the LLM did not provide grounding.

decision.description: A confirmed decision reached during the meeting.

id.description: Stable identifier within the document. Recommended format: 'dec_NNNN'.

context.description: Optional rationale or surrounding context that explains why the decision was made.

source_segment_ref.description: Transcript segment IDs that support this decision. Same role as in action_items.

participant.description: A meeting participant. Used to anchor speaker_id references in transcript.segments.

id.description: Stable identifier within the document. Recommended format: 'p_NNNN' or a diarizer-provided label (e.g. 'SPEAKER_00').

name.description: Display name. May be a real name, an inferred name, or a placeholder like 'Speaker 1'.

transcriptionProvenance.description: Identification of the ASR engine and model used.

model_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the model weights file. Recommended for reproducibility, optional in 0.1.0.

llmProvenance.description: Identification of the LLM and runtime used for a generation step.

model_name.description: Model identifier as used by the runtime (e.g. 'qwen2.5:14b', 'llama3.1:8b').

model_digest.description: Runtime-reported content digest for the model (e.g. Ollama's sha256 digest). Anchors which exact weights were used.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature used for this step.
